name: payba3
purpose: Framework-neutral payment provider integration surface for server-side apps, IDE agents, automation workers, and LLM tool wrappers.
integrationFlow: Read agents.md when generating agent, IDE, MCP, or tool-wrapper code.
Read the provider-specific llms file for every requested provider.
Use createPayba3 with explicit provider config or environment variables.
Expose narrow tool actions instead of exposing raw provider clients to end users.
Verify webhooks and transaction status server-side before delivering value.
securityRules: Never expose provider secrets, JWT client assertions, bearer tokens, access tokens, refresh tokens, or webhook secrets to client-side code.
Never ask an LLM to invent provider credentials or test live money movement with real customer data.
Keep provider keys in environment variables or a server-side secret manager.
Use least-privilege tool actions with explicit JSON schemas.
Require human approval for live transfers, refunds, debits, or destructive payment actions unless the host application has its own approval policy.
Log stable references and provider names, not secrets or full request bodies containing sensitive identity/payment data.
